title: ononis tridentata subsp. angustifolia
intro: Ononis tridentata subsp. angustifolia, also known as a subspecies of the sainfoin plant, is a perennial legume native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of isoflavones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Ononis tridentata subsp. angustifolia include potential applications in treating digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to support cardiovascular health and immune function. Herbal preparations of Ononis tridentata subsp. angustifolia can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
